title: 10 best herbal creams for stress

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to anxiety and depression by reducing inflammation and free radical damage in the body.

GABA-enhancing properties: Lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, helping to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality.

Calming and sedative effects: Lemon balm's essential oils, such as citral and geraniol, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce feelings of stress, anxiety, and restlessness, promoting a sense of calm and tranquility.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with stress because it contains rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds that promote relaxation and calmness. The herb's natural sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and promote a good night's sleep. Additionally, lemon balm cream's cooling sensation can help soothe and calm the mind and body, providing a sense of tranquility and relaxation. This can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing stress and anxiety. Regular use can lead to a more balanced and peaceful state.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps alleviate stress by acting as a natural anxiolytic and promoting relaxation by reducing cortisol levels in the body.
Linalool: A terpene that has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress by promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
Geranial: A terpene that has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety by promoting a sense of relaxation and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has a calming and anti-inflammatory effect.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems also contain rosmarinic acid, contributing to their stress-relieving properties in creams.
Roots: Roots of Melissa officinalis are believed to have a soothing effect, making them a popular choice for stress relief creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves by picking fresh green foliage from mature stems in the morning.
Clean the leaves by gently washing them with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Infuse the melissa officinalis leaves in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a beeswax and shea butter base in a 1:1:1 ratio to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: GABA-stimulating compounds: Valerian cream contains GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), which is a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation, and has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels.

Adaptogenic effects: Valerian root has adaptogenic properties, meaning it helps the body adapt to stressors and promotes balance in the body's response to stress, leading to improved emotional resilience.

Sedative and anti-inflammatory compounds: Valerian cream contains sedative and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe nervous tension, reduce muscle cramps and spasms, and promote a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with stress because of its unique ability to promote relaxation and calmness. The herb contains valepotriates, which interact with the brain's GABA receptors, producing a soothing effect on the nervous system. This leads to a decrease in anxiety and tension, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. As a result, people who use valerian creams often experience improved mood, reduced stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isovalerenol: This sesquiterpene alcohol helps with stress by acting as a GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene acid helps with stress by acting as an inhibitor of GABA reuptake, allowing GABA to accumulate in the brain and promoting a calming effect.
Valerenol: This sesquiterpene alcohol helps with stress by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, similar to isovalerenol.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are used to make creams for stress due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound with sedative and calming properties.
Leaves: Valeriana officinalis leaves are used to make creams for stress due to their content of valerenic acid and other active compounds that contribute to their calming effects.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Valeriana officinalis are used to make creams for stress due to their high concentration of valepotriates, which are known for their anxiolytic and sedative properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water for at least 2 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots with 500 ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c to 70°c for 4 hours or until the roots have broken down.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate the roots from the oil.
Whip 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter into the oil mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Passionflower alkaloids: Maypop cream contains passionflower alkaloids, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress by binding to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calmness.
Lavender oil: The presence of lavender oil in maypop creams can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation by reducing cortisol levels, improving sleep quality, and calming the nervous system.

GABA: Maypop cream often includes GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, relaxation, and anxiety response. By increasing GABA levels, maypop creams can promote a sense of calmness and reduce stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with stress because they contain a high concentration of the active compound passiflorine. This natural sedative works by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. As a result, Passiflora incarnata creams can help alleviate symptoms of stress and promote a good night's sleep. Additionally, their calming properties can soothe the mind and body, making them a popular natural remedy for stress relief and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is a beta-carboline alkaloid that helps with stress by inhibiting the activity of stress-inducing neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine.
Isovitexin: Isovitexin is a flavonoid that helps with stress by having anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects, which can contribute to a sense of calmness and relaxation.
Hesperidin: Hesperidin is a flavanone glycoside that helps with stress by having a calming effect on the nervous system and reducing the production of stress hormones like cortisol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other active compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Stems: The stems are used for their flavonoid and alkaloid content, which helps in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
Fruits: The fruits are used due to their rich content of vitamins, minerals, and flavonoids that contribute to the calming and soothing effects in stress relief creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in a single layer at 50c for 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine 200 grams of the dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves with 400 milliliters of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture at a temperature of 40c for 6 hours to infuse the oil with the passiflora incarnata's medicinal properties.
Strain and filter the mixture through a cheesecloth and pour the herbal infused oil into a glass container for storage.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce anxiety and stress by interacting with serotonin and dopamine receptors, which can lead to a calming effect on the nervous system.
Hypericin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe and calm irritated skin and muscles caused by stress, while also protecting against oxidative damage.
Flavonoids and phenolic acids: Act as natural sedatives and can help to regulate cortisol levels, reducing the negative effects of chronic stress on the body.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with stress because it contains hyperforin and hypericin, active compounds that support the brain's natural mood-regulating processes. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation by soothing the nervous system. Additionally, it may help regulate the body's response to stress, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. As a natural remedy, it offers a non-invasive approach to managing stress and promoting overall mental health.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that helps with stress by acting as a serotonin reuptake inhibitor, increasing serotonin levels in the brain and promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.

Hypericin: A naphthodianthrone derivative that helps with stress by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, which can help regulate mood and reduce anxiety.

Flavonoids (especially rutin and quercetin): A class of plant-derived compounds that help with stress by acting as antioxidants, reducing inflammation, and modulating the body's response to stress, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, such as hyperforin and hypericin, which are known for their anxiolytic and stress-relieving properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum contain a higher concentration of flavonoids, making them a popular choice for stress-relieving creams.
Stems: The stems of the plant also contain flavonoids, which contribute to their anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for stress relief creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh flowers of hypericum perforatum at peak flowering stage early morning to ensure highest potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to preserve medicinal properties.
Steep 10 grams of dried flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove plant material.
Mix 10 grams of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-Anxiolytic Properties: Herbal chamomile creams contain apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. This helps alleviate stress by calming the nervous system.

Sedative and Calming Effects: Chamomile's flavonoids, such as quercetin, have a sedative effect on the body, helping to calm the mind and body, leading to better sleep quality and reduced stress levels.

Inflammation Reduction: Chamomile creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol, which helps reduce inflammation in the skin and body, alleviating stress-related skin issues like acne, rosacea, or eczema.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with stress because of its calming and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. When applied topically, chamomile creams can help to calm the mind and body, reducing muscle tension and promoting a sense of calm. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing stress and anxiety, helping to promote a restful night's sleep and overall sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, helps with stress by acting as an anxiolytic agent, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Matricaria chamomilla flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Matricaria chamomilla, including apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin, help with stress by modulating the body's inflammatory response and reducing oxidative stress.
Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, helps with stress by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, which contribute to their calming and soothing effects.
Seeds: They contain chamazulene, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and stress-relieving properties.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml of oil with 5g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a saucepan over low heat stirring occasionally.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture stirring until well combined.
Pour the cream mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Linalool: This terpene has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.
Lavonin: This compound has been found to exhibit sedative and calming properties, helping to reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calmness in individuals experiencing stress.
Cicloartenol: This saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to mitigate the negative physiological effects of chronic stress, such as inflammation and oxidative damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with stress because of its calming and soothing properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, promote relaxation and reduce anxiety levels. These creams can help slow down heart rate and lower blood pressure, creating a sense of calmness and tranquility. By applying lavender creams to the skin, individuals can experience a calming effect, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels, promoting overall well-being and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool acts as a natural anxiolytic, reducing anxiety and stress by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate has a sedative effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting a sense of calmness and improving sleep quality.
Limonene: Limonene has a mood-boosting effect, reducing stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving overall mental well-being.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and sedative properties, which help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Leaves: They contain similar properties to the flowers, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help soothe and calm the mind and body.
Buds: They are used for their delicate, floral scent and potential stress-relieving properties, which are often more pronounced than in the mature flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning to preserve their potency and fragrance.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven at 100°f for 2 hours to prevent moisture damage.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 160°f for 2 hours to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to remove excess plant material.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Helps to reduce oxidative stress by neutralizing free radicals, which can contribute to feelings of anxiety and tension.
Adaptogenic property: Allows the body to adapt to stressors more effectively, reducing symptoms like irritability and fatigue associated with chronic stress.
Ceramide-regulating property: May help improve skin's natural barrier function, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can be beneficial for reducing stress-related skin issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with stress because of its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract has been found to improve blood flow to the brain, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. Its ability to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress also contributes to its stress-relieving effects. Additionally, ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and fatigue, promoting a feeling of well-being and balance in the body. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote mental clarity and reduce stress.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce stress by acting as antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals in the body and protecting against cell damage, which can contribute to feelings of anxiety and fatigue.
Bilobalide: This terpene has a calming effect, reducing stress by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and glutamate, which play a role in regulating mood and emotional response.
Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stress-related inflammation in the body, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to have a positive effect on stress and anxiety.
Seeds: They are used in some stress relief creams due to their rich content of ginkgolic acids and bilobalide, which have been known for their potential stress-relieving properties.
Roots: They are sometimes used in herbal remedies for stress relief due to their potential adaptogenic properties and high content of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Mix 20g of ginkgo biloba extract with 100g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure even melting.
Add 50g of sweet almond oil and 20g of coconut oil to the mixture.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes to combine the ingredients evenly.
Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal teas like Ashwagandha, Ginseng, and Holy Basil contain adaptogenic compounds that help the body respond to stress by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.
Amino acid content: Teas like Green Tea, Peppermint, and Chamomile are rich in amino acids like L-Theanine, which helps regulate brain waves and promote a sense of calm and focus.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas like Rosehip, Lavender, and Lemon Balm contain antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by daily life, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting overall well-being.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with stress because it contains a wealth of antioxidants and flavonoids. These compounds have been shown to promote relaxation and calmness by reducing cortisol levels and inflammation in the body. The soothing properties of Camellia sinensis creams also help to quiet the mind and promote a sense of balance, making it an excellent natural remedy for managing stress and anxiety. Regular use can lead to a more peaceful and centered state of being.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds help alleviate stress by reducing inflammation and improving mood by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin.
Theaflavins: These flavonoids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help mitigate stress by protecting the body from oxidative damage and promoting relaxation.

L-theanine: This non-protein amino acid has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress levels by promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and regulating the body's response to stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of camellia oil, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for stress relief.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain saponins, which are known for their ability to calm and relax the skin, reducing stress and anxiety.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of camellia sinensis leaves, clean them thoroughly with water, and dry them in a dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried camellia sinensis leaves with 4 cups of distilled water in a large pot and bring to a boil for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of the camellia sinensis infusion with 1/2 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously until it thickens and reaches a temperature of 180°f, then pour it into a glass jar and let it cool.

remedy_properties: Lavender oil: Known for its calming effects, lavender oil helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation by regulating the body's response to stress hormones.

Passionflower extract: Passionflower has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to its ability to bind to GABA receptors in the brain, helping to calm nervous activity and promote a sense of well-being.
Chamomile essential oil: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to soothe an overactive mind and body, reducing symptoms of stress and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia x europaea, also known as lime, creams helps with stress because of its calming properties. The extract of the Tilia flower contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system. The cream can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it an ideal remedy for individuals experiencing stress and anxiety. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience a sense of calm and tranquility, helping to alleviate stress and promote a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes (specifically, flavonoids and sesquiterpenes): These compounds help reduce stress by promoting relaxation, improving mood, and exerting anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects through their interaction with the brain's GABA receptors.

Alkaloids (specifically, tilidine and tilidine-1-β-D-glucuronide): These compounds contribute to the stress-relieving properties of Tilia x europaea by acting as a natural anxiolytic and sedative, helping to calm the mind and body.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the physical and emotional effects of stress, promoting overall well-being and resilience.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Flowers: They have a calming effect and are often used in herbal teas and creams to promote sleep and reduce stress.
Buds: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them useful in stress-relieving creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia x europaea in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Steep 50g of dried leaves in 500ml of carrier oil at 70-80 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard.
Mix 20% of the herbal extract with 80% of a moisturizing base and 1% of.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic flavonoids: Hawthorn cream contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's response to stress by normalizing blood pressure, heart rate, and cortisol levels.

Antioxidant polyphenols: The antioxidants in hawthorn cream, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, inflammation, and anxiety, promoting overall mental well-being.

Cardioprotective oligomeric procyanidins: Oligomeric procyanidins (OPCs) in hawthorn cream have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress by improving cardiovascular health, reducing blood pressure, and enhancing blood flow to the brain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with stress because of its adaptogenic properties. The active compounds in hawthorn, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, help the body adapt to stress by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation. These compounds also have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting a sense of well-being. As a result, hawthorn creams can provide relief from stress and promote a balanced state of mind.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate stress by reducing oxidative damage and promoting relaxation.

Triterpenoids (specifically, ursolic acid): This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help mitigate the negative impacts of chronic stress on the body and promote overall well-being.

Phenolic acids (specifically, chlorogenic acid): As a potent antioxidant, chlorogenic acid can help scavenge free radicals and reduce inflammation, contributing to a sense of calm and reduced stress levels.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress.
Flowers: Used for their sedative and calming effects, which help reduce stress and anxiety.
Buds: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate the negative effects of stress on the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers on a sunny morning in late summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks.
Infuse 1 cup of dried plant material in 4 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 hours of preparation time.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to make the cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with stress because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation and calmness. The calming properties of herbs like chamomile, lavender, and passionflower work together to soothe an anxious mind and body. As we apply these creams to our skin, the herbal essences are absorbed into the bloodstream, releasing endorphins that help ease tension and promote a sense of serenity. With regular use, these grape creams can become a trusted ally in managing stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Vitamins A, C, and E: These antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to stress-induced oxidative damage, promoting overall relaxation and reducing anxiety symptoms.

Grapeseed extract: Rich in polyphenols, grapeseed extract has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety by modulating neurotransmitters like GABA and serotonin.
Lavender oil: The calming scent of lavender oil can help reduce cortisol levels, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, all of which are essential for managing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the grape plant contain compounds that have been shown to have anti-anxiety and calming effects, making them an effective ingredient in reducing stress.
Barks: The barks of the grape plant contain tannins, which have been found to have a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate symptoms of stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality vitis vinifera leaves and stems according to their growth stage and weather conditions carefully.
Dry the harvested plant material in shade or using a food dehydrator at low heat for 24 hours precisely.
Infuse one part of dried vitis vinifera material with two parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 4-6 weeks slowly.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids promptly.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:1:0.5, then whip until creamy and smooth within 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with stress because they contain a blend of calming herbs that work in harmony to soothe the mind and body. The valerian root, for instance, has a sedative effect that promotes relaxation, while the lavender extract calms the nerves and reduces anxiety. As these herbs are absorbed into the skin, they release a sense of tranquility and calmness, helping to alleviate stress and promote a restful night's sleep. With regular use, users may find themselves feeling more centered and composed, even in the most chaotic of situations.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow creams contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress by promoting relaxation and tranquility.

Saponins: Saponins present in marshmallow creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce cortisol levels (the "stress hormone") and alleviate symptoms of stress, such as fatigue and irritability.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root (often used in combination with marshmallow), has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, helping to calm the mind and body, reduce stress, and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, the roots of the marshmallow plant are excellent for soothing and calming the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by stress.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain high concentrations of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to alleviate tension and promote relaxation when applied topically.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root (althaea officinalis) for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Dry the harvested roots using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours.
Combine 10g of dried marshmallow root with 20ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard solids.
Add 5ml of beeswax to the infused oil and stir until melted then fill sterilized jars and store at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with stress because they contain soothing and calming properties that can calm the mind and body. The natural ingredients in herbal oats creams, such as chamomile and lavender, have a profound effect on reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. By applying the cream to the skin, these calming agents are absorbed into the bloodstream, helping to quiet the nervous system and promote a sense of tranquility. This can lead to a reduction in stress levels, allowing individuals to feel more grounded and centered.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal oat creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the skin caused by stress and promote relaxation.

Soothing and Calming: The natural moisturizing properties of oats, combined with herbs like chamomile and lavender, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting a sense of calm.

Antioxidant: Oats contain antioxidants that help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall well-being and reducing the negative impact of chronic stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it effective in soothing stressed skin.
Rhizomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itchiness caused by stress.
Bark: Provides a calming effect when used topically, helping to regulate cortisol levels and alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Measure 200g of dried avena sativa flower heads and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered avena sativa with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler and stir occasionally for 10 minutes.
Add 20g of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully dissolved, then heat the mixture for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat source and let it cool slightly before whipping the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes or until desired consistency is achieved.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and label them, allowing them to set at room temperature for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with stress because they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to soothe and calm the mind and body. The calming properties of elder cream can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and overwhelm, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can help to nourish and rejuvenate the skin, further contributing to a sense of well-being and reduced stress levels.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Elderberry's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to stress-related inflammation and oxidative damage in the body.
Adaptogenic compounds: Elder extracts contain adaptogens like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help the body adapt to stress by reducing cortisol levels, promoting relaxation, and improving mood.

Amino acid-rich peptides: Elderberry creams often contain amino acid-rich peptides that can help regulate neurotransmitters like GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, leading to reduced anxiety and improved stress resilience.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds, making them effective in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Containing soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm nervous tension and reduce symptoms of stress-related insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to local regulations guidelines.
Combine 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra herbs with 100 ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Prepare the infusion by steeping the herb mixture in a double boiler for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Whip the filtered oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil at a ratio of 1:1 to create a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with stress because of its ability to stimulate blood flow and increase oxygenation to the brain, promoting mental clarity and focus. The herb's essential oils have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and tension. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the mind and body, leaving you feeling relaxed and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to feelings of anxiety and fatigue, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to stress and anxiety, and can also improve circulation, promoting relaxation and reducing tension.

Essential oil content: The essential oils present in rosemary creams, such as camphor, bornyl acetate, and α-pinene, have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots help soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground rosemary powder with 8 ounces of distilled water in a double boiler and heat it for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solids and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream has reached a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with stress because of its natural anxiolytic properties, which promote relaxation and calmness. The herbal extract contains flavonoids and volatile oils that work synergistically to reduce anxiety levels by regulating serotonin and GABA neurotransmitters. As a result, fennel cream application can lead to a sense of tranquility, easing muscle tension and promoting better sleep. Additionally, its calming effects can help alleviate symptoms of restlessness, irritability, and fatigue often associated with stress, making it an effective natural remedy for managing emotional well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic compounds: Fennel creams contain anxiolytic compounds such as anethole and limonene, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

Muscle relaxants: The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of fennel's essential oils can help to relax tense muscles and alleviate physical symptoms of stress such as headaches and muscle cramps.
Adaptogenic effects: Fennel's adaptogenic properties help the body to adapt to stress by regulating cortisol levels, promoting a sense of balance and reducing feelings of overwhelm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are calming and soothing, making them an excellent choice for reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation in stress-relieving creams.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have a mild sedative effect, which helps to calm the mind and body, making them ideal for use in creams designed to alleviate stress and promote tranquility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in late summer or early fall when mature.
Grind 50 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 30 grams of coconut oil and 1 gram of ground foeniculum vulgare seeds in a double boiler at medium heat.
Stir the mixture until melted and smooth, taking approximately 15-20 minutes, then remove from heat.
Pour the cream into glass jars and let cool to room temperature before storing in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with stress because they contain compounds that have a profound impact on the body's natural response to anxiety. The cream's active ingredients, including beta-carotene and flavonoids, work synergistically to calm the nervous system, reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation. Additionally, stinging nettle has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe tense muscles and joints, further alleviating physical manifestations of stress. By addressing both mental and physical aspects of anxiety, herbal stinging nettle creams offer a natural and effective way to manage stress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate feelings of anxiety and tension associated with stress.
Adaptogenic saponins: Nettle creams contain adaptogenic saponins, such as beta-sitosterol, which can help the body adapt to stressful situations by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant flavonoids: Nettle creams are rich in antioxidant flavonoids like quercetin, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by environmental toxins and daily living, further contributing to feelings of calm and reduced anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing stress-related inflammation and promoting relaxation.

Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and promote calmness, making them a great addition to medicinal creams for stress relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves measuring 200g and dry them using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using an herb grinder and store it in an airtight container.
Mix 100g of powdered nettle with 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 10g of olive oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat it at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to melt the ingredients.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal them tightly, and let them cool before storing them in a dark cupboard for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with stress because they contain natural essential oils that have a calming effect on the mind and body. The menthol in peppermint oil can help to reduce muscle tension, ease headaches, and promote relaxation. Additionally, the creamy texture of herbal peppermint creams provides a soothing and comforting sensation when applied topically. This combination of aromatherapy and tactile comfort can help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress, allowing for a sense of tranquility and well-being.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to relax tense muscles, soothe headaches, and ease mental fatigue caused by stress, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint, such as menthone and limonene, have a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress levels by promoting a sense of tranquility and peace.

Magnesium: Peppermint creams often contain magnesium, which plays a crucial role in relaxation and stress relief by calming the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms, and promoting better sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like rosmarinic acid, peppermint roots help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain high levels of menthol and menthone, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and can help alleviate stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh peppermint leaves for infusion ensuring crisp texture and aroma retention.
Combine dried peppermint leaves with distilled water in a heat-resistant container at a 1:10 ratio.
Heat the mixture gently over low medium flame for 15 minutes allowing flavors and oils to mingle.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Mix 20% of the peppermint infusion with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler whipping until smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw creams helps with stress because they contain the unique properties of Harpagophytum procumbens, a herb known for its anti-inflammatory and anxiety-reducing benefits. When applied topically, the cream can help calm the mind and body, easing feelings of tension and anxiety. The cream's active compounds also work to reduce cortisol levels, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle fatigue caused by prolonged periods of stress. Regular use can lead to a sense of calm and tranquility, making it an effective natural remedy for managing daily stress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by stress, anxiety, and tension.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in devil's claw cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to stress-related conditions like anxiety and depression.

Anxiolytic effects: Devil's claw has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, likely due to its ability to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, which can help regulate mood and reduce feelings of anxiety and stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive glycosides, the rhyzomes have a calming effect on the nervous system, alleviating stress and promoting mental well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest harpagophytum procumbens roots and dry them according to the traditional method for six hours under shade.
Grind dried harpagophytum procumbens roots into a fine powder using mortar and pestle and measure 50 grams of powder.
Combine 50 grams of harpagophytum procumbens root powder with 200 milliliters of coconut oil and 100 milliliters of beeswax in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for one hour, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully dissolved and the mixture reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Pour the hot herbal cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool at room temperature before storing in the refrigerator for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for stress are topical preparations that combine the soothing effects of herbs with the moisturizing benefits of creams, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and calmness. The best herbal creams for stress include Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Passiflora incarnata, Melissa officinalis, Avena sativa, Hypericum perforatum, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Centella asiatica, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, stress can lead to sleep disturbances, decreased productivity, and strained relationships, ultimately impacting one's overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stress that are treated with herbal creams are anxiety, insomnia, and skin irritations such as eczema and acne. Herbal creams containing ingredients like lavender, chamomile, and passionflower help soothe the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting relaxation. Topical applications of these herbs can also calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with stress is Lavender and Chamomile. Lavender cream calms the mind and body, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Chamomile cream soothes the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of calm. When combined, they create a powerful synergy that can help alleviate stress and promote a peaceful night's sleep. This natural duo can be applied topically to the skin, providing a safe and effective way to manage stress and promote overall well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in medicine, a systematic review and meta-analysis of 21 articles found that lavender significantly reduced individuals' stress. The study, which included a sample size of 791 in the intervention group and 804 in the control group, found that the use of lavender resulted in a significant decrease of 0.63 ± 0.13 (95% CI) in stress scores compared to the control group (P < 0.001). This suggests that herbal creams containing lavender may be a useful addition to stress management programs, particularly for student groups, by helping to reduce stress levels and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with stress because they contain valerenic acid, a natural compound that has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The cream's soothing properties calm the mind and body, allowing individuals to unwind and feel more centered. By reducing muscle tension and promoting a sense of calm, Valeriana officinalis creams can help alleviate stress and anxiety, making them a popular natural remedy for those looking to manage their stress levels and improve their overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It has a sedative effect, helping to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Isovaleric acid: It has a calming effect, contributing to reduced stress levels by inhibiting the activity of stress-related neurotransmitters and promoting a sense of relaxation.

Valeranone: It has a sedative and anxiolytic effect, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety by interacting with the body's GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) system, which regulates the activity of neurons and promotes relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is the most commonly used part of Valeriana officinalis due to its high concentration of valerenic acids, which are responsible for its sedative and calming properties.
Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are also used to create creams due to their ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels.
Roots: Valerian root is used in creams for stress relief because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have a balancing effect on the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for uniform consistency.
Mix the ground valeriana officinalis powder with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2:3 by weight to create a smooth paste.
Combine the valeriana officinalis paste with distilled water and heat it gently in a double boiler for 10 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with stress because they contain calming properties that promote relaxation. The herb's soothing scent and active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to calm the mind and body. These compounds have been shown to reduce anxiety and promote better sleep quality, making it easier to manage daily stress. By applying the cream to the skin, the calming effects of the herb are absorbed, providing a natural and effective way to calm the mind and body.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with stress by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to stress relief by exhibiting anxiolytic and sedative properties, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Caffeic acid: Plays a role in stress management by acting as an antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the body's response to stress.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their calming and soothing properties to reduce stress and anxiety.
Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to help alleviate stress and promote relaxation.
Essential oils extracted from Flowers: Used for their sedative and mood-enhancing effects to help reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when the petals are fully opened.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried flowers in 1 cup of carrier oil like sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Strain the infused oil after 2-3 weeks and combine it with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter.
Whip the mixture for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and creamy, taking about 10-15 minutes to set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with stress because they contain an extract of the passionflower plant, a natural herb known for its calming properties. The active compounds in passionflower, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, work to regulate the body's stress response by interacting with neurotransmitters like GABA and serotonin. This helps to reduce feelings of anxiety and promote relaxation, leading to a decrease in stress levels. By applying a topical cream, the beneficial effects can be absorbed directly into the skin.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Isophorone derivatives: These constituents have sedative properties, which help to slow down brain activity and reduce stress levels.
Harman alkaloids: These compounds have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation without causing dependence.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their leaves are commonly used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have a calming effect on the body.
Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata are utilized for their rich concentration of vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin C and potassium, which contribute to relaxation.
Barks: The barks of the plant are also used due to their high content of flavonoids and lignans, which have been shown to have sedative properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 30c for 24 hours in a dehydrator.
Combine 1g of dried passiflora incarnata flowers with 10g of sweet almond oil in a small bowl.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 30c for 2 hours then strain through a cheesecloth.
Whip the infused oil with 5g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with stress because they contain the extracts of the calming herb lemon balm, which has a long history of use in traditional medicine. The active compounds in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. By applying lemon balm cream to the skin, the calming properties can be absorbed, helping to soothe the mind and body, and promoting a sense of calm and tranquility. This natural approach can be a valuable tool in managing stress and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, helping to reduce oxidative stress and anxiety-related symptoms.
Linalool: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-induced anxiety.
Geranial: Demonstrates a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and promoting a sense of calmness and well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are widely used in stress-relieving creams due to their high content of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have calming properties.
Flowers: Melissa officinalis flowers are used in creams to help alleviate stress and anxiety due to their high concentration of essential oils that promote relaxation and reduce mental fatigue.

Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are used in creams to take advantage of their bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup dried melissa officinalis flowers and 1/2 cup carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steam distill the melissa officinalis flowers to release their essential oil for 2 hours.
Combine 1/4 cup of the distilled essential oil with 1/2 cup beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax has melted and the cream has reached a smooth consistency after 10 minutes.
Pour the melissa officinalis cream into jars and allow them to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with stress because they contain the calming properties of oat kernel extract. This natural ingredient is known to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety by slowing down the heart rate and promoting a sense of calmness. Avena sativa creams work by regulating the body's response to stress, helping to balance the nervous system and promote a restful night's sleep. This can lead to an overall sense of well-being and reduced stress levels.
remedy_constituents: Avenacoside: Helps with stress by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting relaxation through its anxiolytic properties.
Lignans: Contributes to stress relief by modulating the body's cortisol levels and reducing anxiety.
Beta-sitosterol: Plays a role in stress management by exerting anti-inflammatory effects and promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Avena sativa seeds are used to make creams for stress due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Avena sativa roots are used to make creams for stress due to their high content of beta-sitosterol, a plant sterol that has been shown to have anxiolytic effects.
Leaves: Avena sativa leaves are used to make creams for stress due to their high content of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried avena sativa in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes then let steep.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids immediately.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of vegetable glycerin and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Gradually add 2 tablespoons of the infused avena sativa liquid to the double boiler mixture while stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with stress because they contain a high concentration of hypericin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have anxiolytic properties. This natural compound helps to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and restlessness. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, Hypericum perforatum creams can help individuals cope with stress and promote a sense of well-being. Regular use may also improve mood and reduce symptoms of depression.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It has a strong antioxidant effect, which helps to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to feelings of anxiety and stress.

N-Acylated flavonoids (e.g., hyperoside, quercetin, and kaempferol): These compounds have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress and anxiety by inhibiting the activity of neurotransmitters associated with stress.

Flavonoids (e.g., rutin and kaempferol): They have anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and GABA, promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and hyperforin, which have anti-anxiety and antidepressant properties.
Leaves: They contain hyperforin and hypericin, which are responsible for their calming and stress-relieving effects.
Roots: They are used for their ability to reduce stress and anxiety, thanks to their high content of flavonoids and glycosides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at the peak of blooming on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to preserve their medicinal properties.
Steep 2-3 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Mix the resulting oil infusion with 20-30 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with stress because they contain natural compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, making it easier to manage stress. The warming and soothing sensation of ginger creams can also help to calm the mind and body, promoting a sense of well-being and calmness. This can be especially beneficial during times of high stress or anxiety.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress-induced inflammation in the body and promote relaxation.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate stress and promote a sense of calm.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and stress levels by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Ginger due to their high concentration of active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and stress-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Ginger are also widely used for their calming effects, which can help reduce anxiety and stress.
Buds: Ginger buds are used in some creams for their unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes in 100ml of carrier oil like coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth to separate the solid particles from the liquid.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt at 160-180°f.
Gradually add 20ml of the infused oil to the melted beeswax mixture and stir until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with stress because of its ability to promote relaxation and calmness. The antioxidant properties in Ginkgo biloba help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to feelings of anxiety and tension. By improving blood flow to the brain, Ginkgo biloba creams can enhance cognitive function and reduce stress-related symptoms. Additionally, its calming effects can help to regulate mood and reduce stress hormones, leading to a sense of tranquility and well-being. This can help individuals manage stress more effectively.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and promoting relaxation.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has a sedative effect, helping to calm the mind and body, reducing anxiety and stress.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing stress-related inflammation and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to their stress-relieving properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain a high amount of bilobalide, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on stress and anxiety.

Roots: The roots of the Ginkgo biloba tree are also used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have a beneficial effect on stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of ginkgo biloba extract with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved and melted in about 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat source and let the mixture cool slightly, then add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it becomes light and fluffy, about 5 minutes, until it reaches a desired consistency.
Transfer the herbal ginkgo biloba cream to a container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams helps with stress because they contain the herb's active compounds, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the mind and body. The herb is believed to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety by balancing the nervous system and regulating cortisol levels. Additionally, it's thought to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by stress. By providing a sense of calm and relaxation, Centella asiatica creams can help individuals manage stress and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help reduce stress by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis and improving mood through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Phenolic acids: Contribute to stress relief by scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting relaxation due to their ability to interact with GABA receptors.
Asiatic acid: Assist in stress management by modulating the HPA axis, reducing cortisol levels, and improving cognitive function through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which contribute to their stress-relieving properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Centella asiatica are rich in asiatic acid, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate stress.
Stems: The stems of Centella asiatica also contain triterpenoids and other active compounds, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of dried centella asiatica leaves in 1l of boiling water for 20-30 minutes to extract the herbs' properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids and reserving 200ml of infusion.
Combine the reserved infusion with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture over low heat or using a microwave for 10-15 seconds at a time stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the warm mixture into sterilized containers and let cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-40 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with stress because they contain licorice root extract, known for its adaptogenic properties. This means it can help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in the body's response to it. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can also soothe and calm the mind and body, reducing feelings of anxiety and tension. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide a natural and effective way to manage stress and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with stress by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, thereby reducing the physical symptoms of stress and promoting relaxation.
Flavonoids: They help with stress by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-anxiety properties, which contribute to reducing stress-induced inflammation and anxiety.
Saponins: They help with stress by possessing adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adapt to stress and promote balance in the body's physiological response to stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and stress-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antioxidant and adaptogenic properties, which help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used, as they contain compounds that have been shown to have a calming effect and reduce anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 25 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in 500 milliliters of hot water for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Combine the infused liquid with 10 milliliters of jojoba oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.
